analysis.explanation: Detected document as Technical Document because the following keywords were found: implementation, api, database

rejected_entities.reason: Likely person name misclassified as organization

Drizzle.sentence: Pick a query approach (no specific ORM required) Decide upfront how you'll talk to Postgres: raw SQL ( pg ), Drizzle, Prisma, Knex, or similar.

Heroku.sentence: Account on one of: Render, Railway, Fly.io, Vercel (paired with hosted Postgres), Heroku, or your own AWS / GCP / Azure Free tier or trial credits cover both a small Node service and a small Postgres database.

Mastra.sentence: Mastra Project Setup The assignment must be built using the Mastra SDK.

Scaffold a sandbox project: npm create mastra@latest During the wizard, select at minimum: Agents and Tools (Workflows is optional for this assignment) Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 1 Pick a model provider you have a key for: OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Boot the dev server: cd <project> npm run dev Open the Mastra playground in the browser (the CLI prints the URL on startup) and confirm the sample agent responds Bookmark the docs you will actually need during the build: Quickstart createTool reference Agent reference Adding Mastra to an Express server Text-to-SQL template (closest example) ✅ 3.

LLM tool calling: how the model emits a tool call, how the runtime executes it, how the result is fed back Mastra primitives: createTool , Agent , the agent loop Zod schemas: validating tool inputs from a non-deterministic caller Grounding: why every number must come from a tool query, not the model's prose Multi-step orchestration: handling questions that need two or more tool calls (compare, rank, derive, join) Express 5: a single POST /ask route, JSON request and response SQL fundamentals: WHERE , GROUP BY , aggregates, dates, joins.

Final Verification Before You Start Mastra sandbox project boots and the sample agent answers a question Model API key works from a script — non-zero token count returned Local Postgres is running and accessible; provue_tara database exists and accepts a connection from psql or a client You can deploy a "hello world" Node service to your chosen platform and hit it from your browser You have a hosted Postgres connection string ready for the deployed environment An empty submission repo is on GitHub with .gitignore and a stub README.md All three data/sample_*/ snapshots open cleanly in your editor; you have eyeballed the variation between them You have read the assignment brief end-to-end at least once and noted the multi-snapshot grading model and the reliability rules Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 5 You have skimmed DESIGN.md expectations — schema, indexes, formulas for spend, net spend, merchant matching, recurring detection, fund period return, holding realised return You know which model provider, which deployment target (app + Postgres), and roughly how many tools and tables you intend to build 🎯 What You Will Submit A GitHub repo (or zip) containing your schema, ingest script, code, README.md , DESIGN.md , and an eval script A public URL where POST /ask returns { "answer": "..." } for any question, backed by a deployed Postgres Observability evidence: logs, trace output, or screenshots showing one successful run and one handled no-data / failure run An eval script with at least 12 questions covering single lookup, date filtering, refunds, merchant aliases, transfers, category comparison, recurring subscriptions, no-data cases, fund period returns, and realised returns on holdings 📞 Stuck?

Postman.sentence: Local Dev Environment Editor of choice installed: VS Code or Cursor Recommended extensions: TypeScript & JavaScript Language Features (built-in) Prettier — code formatter ESLint GitLens REST Client (for hitting POST /ask while developing) SQLTools or PostgreSQL — for inspecting tables and running queries from the editor A terminal you are comfortable in (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Windows Terminal, etc.) A way to send POST /ask requests: curl, Postman, or the REST Client extension ✅ 8.

Sample_C/.sentence: You see three folders under data/ : sample_a/ , sample_b/ , sample_c/ — each contains transactions.json , funds.json , holdings.json You have opened all three and compared them: different merchants appear in different folders; one folder uses more NEFT-style memos.

Supabase.sentence: Plan for managed Postgres at deploy time Pick a free-tier hosted Postgres for the deployed service: Neon, Supabase, Render Postgres, or Railway Postgres.
Render and Railway bundle both; Vercel needs a separate Postgres (Neon/Supabase).

Vercel.sentence: Account on one of: Render, Railway, Fly.io, Vercel (paired with hosted Postgres), Heroku, or your own AWS / GCP / Azure Free tier or trial credits cover both a small Node service and a small Postgres database.
Render and Railway bundle both; Vercel needs a separate Postgres (Neon/Supabase).

Express 5.sentence: LLM tool calling: how the model emits a tool call, how the runtime executes it, how the result is fed back Mastra primitives: createTool , Agent , the agent loop Zod schemas: validating tool inputs from a non-deterministic caller Grounding: why every number must come from a tool query, not the model's prose Multi-step orchestration: handling questions that need two or more tool calls (compare, rank, derive, join) Express 5: a single POST /ask route, JSON request and response SQL fundamentals: WHERE , GROUP BY , aggregates, dates, joins.

HTTPS.sentence: Node.js and TypeScript Install Node.js Install Node.js v18 or newer (LTS recommended) from https://nodejs.org/ Verify in a terminal: node  version npm  version You should see Node 18+ and npm 9+ printed TypeScript runner You will run TypeScript scripts directly with tsx .

GitHub Repository GitHub account ready (use a professional username — recruiters see it) Git installed and configured: git config  global user.name "Your Name" git config  global user.email "you@example.co SSH key added to GitHub or a personal access token configured for HTTPS Create an empty private repo for your submission (you can flip it public when you submit) Add a .gitignore that excludes node_modules/ , .env , dist/ , and any cached LLM logs Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 3 ✅ 6.

Homebrew.sentence: Pick one path: macOS (Homebrew): brew install postgresql@16 then brew services start postgresql@16 Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 2 Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install postgresql-16 Windows: the installer from postgresql.org Docker (fastest): docker run -d  name provue-pg -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres postgres:16 Verify the server is up: psql  version prints a version, and psql -U postgres -c 'select 1' connects.

Knex.sentence: Pick a query approach (no specific ORM required) Decide upfront how you'll talk to Postgres: raw SQL ( pg ), Drizzle, Prisma, Knex, or similar.

NEFT.sentence: You see three folders under data/ : sample_a/ , sample_b/ , sample_c/ — each contains transactions.json , funds.json , holdings.json You have opened all three and compared them: different merchants appear in different folders; one folder uses more NEFT-style memos.

You have spotted the deliberate complications: refunds (negative amount ), merchant aliases ( Swiggy / Swiggy Instamart / SWIGGY*ORDER / SWIGGY BANGALORE ), self-transfers, missing categories ( uncategorized ), noisy UPI/NEFT memos.

POSTGRES_PASSWORD.sentence: Pick one path: macOS (Homebrew): brew install postgresql@16 then brew services start postgresql@16 Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 2 Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install postgresql-16 Windows: the installer from postgresql.org Docker (fastest): docker run -d  name provue-pg -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres postgres:16 Verify the server is up: psql  version prints a version, and psql -U postgres -c 'select 1' connects.

Prisma.sentence: Pick a query approach (no specific ORM required) Decide upfront how you'll talk to Postgres: raw SQL ( pg ), Drizzle, Prisma, Knex, or similar.

Provue.sentence: Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist This checklist gets your environment ready to build Tara, the finance-research persona described in the Provue Engineering Take-Home assignment.

Scaffold a sandbox project: npm create mastra@latest During the wizard, select at minimum: Agents and Tools (Workflows is optional for this assignment) Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 1 Pick a model provider you have a key for: OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google Boot the dev server: cd <project> npm run dev Open the Mastra playground in the browser (the CLI prints the URL on startup) and confirm the sample agent responds Bookmark the docs you will actually need during the build: Quickstart createTool reference Agent reference Adding Mastra to an Express server Text-to-SQL template (closest example) ✅ 3.
Add the key to a local .env file (which is in .gitignore ) If you cannot get a key: mention it in your README — Provue will help unblock you ✅ 4.

Pick one path: macOS (Homebrew): brew install postgresql@16 then brew services start postgresql@16 Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 2 Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install postgresql-16 Windows: the installer from postgresql.org Docker (fastest): docker run -d  name provue-pg -p 5432:5432 -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres postgres:16 Verify the server is up: psql  version prints a version, and psql -U postgres -c 'select 1' connects.

Create the assignment database Create a database for the assignment: createdb provue_tara # or, via psql: # psql -U postgres -c "CREATE DATABASE provue_tara; Save the connection string somewhere you'll remember (you'll need it in your .env ): DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/provue_tara Confirm a client tool can connect: psql (CLI), pgAdmin, DBeaver, or the VS Code SQLTools extension.

GitHub Repository GitHub account ready (use a professional username — recruiters see it) Git installed and configured: git config  global user.name "Your Name" git config  global user.email "you@example.co SSH key added to GitHub or a personal access token configured for HTTPS Create an empty private repo for your submission (you can flip it public when you submit) Add a .gitignore that excludes node_modules/ , .env , dist/ , and any cached LLM logs Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 3 ✅ 6.
Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 4 You understand the grading model: Provue runs your ingest script against a fourth snapshot you have never seen, then hits POST /ask .

Final Verification Before You Start Mastra sandbox project boots and the sample agent answers a question Model API key works from a script — non-zero token count returned Local Postgres is running and accessible; provue_tara database exists and accepts a connection from psql or a client You can deploy a "hello world" Node service to your chosen platform and hit it from your browser You have a hosted Postgres connection string ready for the deployed environment An empty submission repo is on GitHub with .gitignore and a stub README.md All three data/sample_*/ snapshots open cleanly in your editor; you have eyeballed the variation between them You have read the assignment brief end-to-end at least once and noted the multi-snapshot grading model and the reliability rules Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 5 You have skimmed DESIGN.md expectations — schema, indexes, formulas for spend, net spend, merchant matching, recurring detection, fund period return, holding realised return You know which model provider, which deployment target (app + Postgres), and roughly how many tools and tables you intend to build 🎯 What You Will Submit A GitHub repo (or zip) containing your schema, ingest script, code, README.md , DESIGN.md , and an eval script A public URL where POST /ask returns { "answer": "..." } for any question, backed by a deployed Postgres Observability evidence: logs, trace output, or screenshots showing one successful run and one handled no-data / failure run An eval script with at least 12 questions covering single lookup, date filtering, refunds, merchant aliases, transfers, category comparison, recurring subscriptions, no-data cases, fund period returns, and realised returns on holdings 📞 Stuck?
Setup Checklist — for the Provue Engineering Take-Home: Build a Finance-Research Agent.
Provue Engineering Take-Home — Pre-requisites Setup Checklist 6

typescript.sentence: Node.js and TypeScript Install Node.js Install Node.js v18 or newer (LTS recommended) from https://nodejs.org/ Verify in a terminal: node  version npm  version You should see Node 18+ and npm 9+ printed TypeScript runner You will run TypeScript scripts directly with tsx .
Install it globally (optional but convenient): npm install -g tsx typescript Verify: tsx  version and tsc  version both print a version ✅ 2.

Local Dev Environment Editor of choice installed: VS Code or Cursor Recommended extensions: TypeScript & JavaScript Language Features (built-in) Prettier — code formatter ESLint GitLens REST Client (for hitting POST /ask while developing) SQLTools or PostgreSQL — for inspecting tables and running queries from the editor A terminal you are comfortable in (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Windows Terminal, etc.) A way to send POST /ask requests: curl, Postman, or the REST Client extension ✅ 8.
